content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 19, 1991


Kirk Allen Pessner
Paralegal Administrator
Political Reports Unit
NIELSEN, MERKSAMER, HODGSON,
PARRINELLO & MUELLER
591 Redwood Highway, #4000
Mill Valley, CA 94941

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-336

Dear Mr. Pessner:

This letter confirms the advice I provided to you on behalf
of San Francisco Mayor Art Agnos on July 2, 1991 concerning the
current status of Section 85304 of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act")¹ after the court ruling in Service Employees International
Union, AFL-CIO, et al. v. Fair Political Practices Commission
(1990) 747 F.Supp. 580 ("SEIU"). Please be aware that our advice
is limited to the provisions of the Political Reform Act and local
or federal laws may set stricter standards.

As we discussed in our telephone conversation, the United
States District Court issued an order in the SEIU case which
invalidated the fiscal year contribution limitations of the Act
and certain applications of the transfer ban in Section 85304.²
However, the court did not invalidate the transfer ban of the Act
prohibiting candidates from transferring campaign funds to any
candidate who is subject to any valid contribution limits,
including those limits established by local jurisdictions.

_________________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 The decision became final on January 18, 1991. Please note,
the decision in SEIU is being appealed.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Our File No. A-91-336
Page 2


     In light of these changes in the law, you asked a series of
questions concerning the current application of the transfer ban
to San Francisco's system of local limits.  First you asked
whether the transfer ban applied to officeholder accounts which
are not subject to contribution limits under the San Francisco
Campaign Ordinance.  I advised that where the account was not
subject to a contribution limitation, the transfer ban would not
prohibit California state and local candidates from transferring
campaign funds to the account.  I also cautioned, however, that no
candidate funds transferred to the account should be used to make
contributions to any candidate's campaign account which is subject
to valid limits, including limits of local jurisdictions and
special election limits imposed by the Act.

     You also asked whether the transfer ban applied to federal
candidates or out-of-state candidates.  I advised that federal
candidates and out-of-state candidates were not "candidates" as
defined in the Act and were therefore not subject to the transfer
ban. (Section 82007.)  Please note that the local ordinance may
impose special restrictions or impose a limit on contributions from
such persons.

     Finally, you asked whether a federal candidate could transfer
funds from his federal account to a state candidate, despite the
fact that the federal candidate might also be a state candidate.
I explained that the transfer ban would apply to the candidate's
campaign funds held by the state campaign committee but not the
federal.  However, I also cautioned that where the federal
committee was merely used as a conduit to circumvent the transfer
ban, our advice would be different.

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        By: John W. Wallace
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JWW:aa
